title: Depth Recurrence (3+3 x 2 loops) + HW Optimizations
body:
## Summary

- **Depth recurrence**: 6 unique layers (3 encoder + 3 decoder) looped 2x = 12 effective layers, freeing ~7.5MB of the 16MB budget for wider MLPs or more BigramHash buckets
- **Per-loop conditioning**: Learned scale + bias per (loop, block) so tied layers differentiate passes
- **U-Net preserved**: Skip connections work across effective layers (LIFO), looping within encoder/decoder halves separately
- **LR scaling**: `1/sqrt(num_loops)` for matrix and scalar optimizers

### Training HW optimizations
- Async data prefetch (background thread + separate CUDA stream)
- Pinned memory for H2D transfers
- NCCL tuning for H100 NVLink (`NCCL_NVLS_ENABLE=1`, `NCCL_NET_GDR_LEVEL=5`)
- GPU-resident SWA accumulation (avoids D2H sync per checkpoint)
- `torch.cuda.empty_cache()` after warmup

Built on top of the current #1 submission (10L Int5-MLP, 1.1428 bpb).

## Test plan

- [ ] Run on 1xH100 to verify training completes without errors
- [ ] Verify artifact size < 16MB
- [ ] Run 3 seeds on 8xH100 for statistical significance
- [ ] Compare quantization roundtrip gap (recurrence vs baseline)
- [ ] Confirm backward compat: `NUM_LAYERS=10` produces identical behavior to #1

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body: Closing — no H100 validation results yet. Will reopen once we have 3-seed runs with val_bpb scores.
